content:
# Numbers Chapter 26 — The EMET Bible (Literal)

**Translator:** EMET | **Source:** Leningrad Codex (B19A), pages 179–181 | **Source Images:** `source-texts/hebrew-OT/NUM_026_page_001.jpg`, `NUM_026_page_002.jpg`, `NUM_026_page_003.jpg`
**Secondary Source:** Codex Vaticanus (Vat.gr.1209), pa_0132–0134 | **Source Images:** `source-texts/greek-OT-LXX/NUM_026_vaticanus_page_001.jpg`, `NUM_026_vaticanus_page_002.jpg`, `NUM_026_vaticanus_page_003.jpg`

---

> **MANUSCRIPT OBSERVATION — THE SECOND CENSUS ON THE PLAINS OF MOAB:** Numbers 26 is the second great census chapter of the book, forming a deliberate literary parallel with the first census of chapter 1, taken thirty-eight years earlier at Sinai. This census occurs after the plague of Baal Peor (25:9) that killed twenty-four thousand, and its placement immediately after the Phinehas episode underscores a theological point: YHWH's people must be reconstituted and recounted before they can enter the promised land. The Leningrad Codex presents this chapter across pages 179–181, continuing in the standard three-column Masoretic format. The chapter follows a rigid formulaic pattern for each tribe: "The sons of X: of Y, the clan of the Y-ite..." (לְ...מִשְׁפַּחַת הַ..., le-...mishpakhat ha-...), concluding each tribal unit with a census total. The Hebrew term מִשְׁפָּחָה (mishpakhah, "clan/family") occurs over forty times. The chapter also contains the important narrative digression regarding the daughters of Zelophehad (v. 33), who will become central figures in chapter 27, and the concluding summary of Levitical clans (vv. 57–62) counted separately from the military census. The Vaticanus LXX (pa_0132–0134) follows the MT closely but presents the tribal lists with some minor orthographic variations in proper names. Notably, the LXX verse numbering differs from the MT in several places within this chapter; I follow the MT versification throughout. The grand total of six hundred and one thousand seven hundred and thirty (601,730) represents a slight decrease from the first census total of 603,550 (Num 1:46), theologically signifying that the wilderness generation has indeed perished as YHWH decreed (14:29), yet the nation endures.

---

## YHWH Commands the Second Census (vv. 1–4)

**1** And-it-was [va-yehi] after [akharei] the-plague [ha-maggefah]; and-said [va-yomer] YHWH unto [el] Moses [Mosheh] and-unto [ve-el] Eleazar [El'azar] son of [ben] Aaron [Aharon] the-priest [ha-kohen], saying [le'mor]:

> **Key terms:** וַיְהִי אַחֲרֵי הַמַּגֵּפָה (va-yehi akharei ha-maggefah) = "and-it-was after the plague" — the opening links this chapter directly to the plague of 25:8–9, in which twenty-four thousand died. The commission now goes to Moses and Eleazar (not Aaron, who died at Mount Hor, 20:28). **LXX:** καὶ ἐγένετο μετὰ τὴν πληγήν, καὶ ἐλάλησεν κύριος πρὸς Μωυσῆν καὶ Ελεαζαρ τὸν ἱερέα ("and it happened after the plague, and the Lord spoke to Moses and Eleazar the priest").

**2** "Lift up [se'u] [et] the-head of [rosh] all [kol] the-congregation of [adat] the-Sons of [benei] Israel [Yisra'el], from-a-son of [mi-ben] twenty [esrim] year[s] [shanah] and-upward [va-ma'lah], by-the-house of [le-veit] their-fathers [avotam], all [kol] going-out [yotse'] to-the-host [tsava'] in-Israel [be-Yisra'el]."

> **Key terms:** שְׂאוּ אֶת־רֹאשׁ (se'u et-rosh) = "lift up the head" — the same census idiom as 1:2, linking the two census chapters. מִבֶּן עֶשְׂרִים שָׁנָה וָמָעְלָה (mi-ben esrim shanah va-ma'lah) = "from a son of twenty years and upward" — the same age threshold as the first census, counting military-eligible males. **LXX:** λάβετε τὴν ἀρχὴν πάσης συναγωγῆς υἱῶν Ισραηλ ἀπὸ εἰκοσαετοῦς καὶ ἐπάνω.

**3** And-spoke [va-yedabber] Moses [Mosheh] and-Eleazar [ve-El'azar] the-priest [ha-kohen] [et] them [otam] in-the-plains of [be-arvot] Moab [Mo'av], by [al] the-Jordan of [Yarden] Jericho [Yerekho], saying [le'mor]:

> **Key terms:** בְּעַרְבֹת מוֹאָב (be-arvot Mo'av) = "in the plains of Moab" — the location of the second census, contrasting with "the wilderness of Sinai" in 1:1. עַל יַרְדֵּן יְרֵחוֹ (al Yarden Yerekho) = "by the Jordan of Jericho" — i.e., the Jordan opposite Jericho. This phrase recurs throughout the final chapters of Numbers as the geographical marker for the staging ground before the conquest. **LXX:** ἐπὶ δυσμῶν Μωαβ ἐπὶ τοῦ Ιορδάνου κατὰ Ιεριχω ("in the west of Moab upon the Jordan opposite Jericho").

**4** "From-a-son of [mi-ben] twenty [esrim] year[s] [shanah] and-upward [va-ma'lah]" — as [ka-asher] commanded [tsivvah] YHWH [et] Moses [Mosheh] and-the-Sons of [u-vnei] Israel [Yisra'el], the-ones-going-out [ha-yots'im] from-the-land of [me-erets] Egypt [Mitsrayim]:

> **Key terms:** The verse restates the census qualification and provides a retrospective link: כַּאֲשֶׁר צִוָּה יְהוָה אֶת־מֹשֶׁה (ka-asher tsivvah YHWH et-Mosheh) = "as YHWH commanded Moses." The phrase הַיֹּצְאִים מֵאֶרֶץ מִצְרָיִם (ha-yots'im me-erets Mitsrayim) = "the ones going out from the land of Egypt" — a transitional note connecting to the new generation being counted. **LXX:** ἀπὸ εἰκοσαετοῦς καὶ ἐπάνω, ὃν τρόπον συνέταξεν κύριος τῷ Μωυσῇ.

---

## Reuben — The Firstborn (vv. 5–11)

**5** Reuben [Re'uven], the-firstborn of [bekhor] Israel [Yisra'el] — the-sons of [benei] Reuben [Re'uven]: of-Hanoch [la-Khanokh], the-clan of [mishpakhat] the-Hanochite [ha-Khanokhi]; of-Pallu [le-Fallu], the-clan of [mishpakhat] the-Palluite [ha-Fallu'i];

> **Key terms:** בְּכוֹר יִשְׂרָאֵל (bekhor Yisra'el) = "the firstborn of Israel" — Reuben's status as firstborn is deliberately noted, though his tribe's diminished numbers reflect the forfeiture of the birthright (cf. Gen 49:3–4). מִשְׁפַּחַת (mishpakhat) = "the clan of" — the formulaic term repeated throughout this chapter; from שׁפח, denoting a sub-tribal unit. **LXX:** Ρουβην πρωτότοκος Ισραηλ· υἱοὶ δὲ Ρουβην· Ενωχ, δῆμος τοῦ Ενωχ.

**6** Of-Hezron [le-Khetsron], the-clan of [mishpakhat] the-Hezronite [ha-Khetsroni]; of-Carmi [le-Kharmi], the-clan of [mishpakhat] the-Carmite [ha-Kharmi].

**7** These [elleh] are the-clans of [mishpekhot] the-Reubenite [ha-Re'uveni]; and-were [va-yihyu] their-mustered-ones [pequdeihem] three [shloshah] and-forty [ve-arba'im] thousand [elef] and-seven [ve-sheva'] hundreds [me'ot] and-thirty [u-shloshim].

> **Census total: forty-three thousand seven hundred and thirty (43,730).** Compared to the first census (46,500 in 1:21), Reuben has decreased by 2,770.

**8** And-the-sons of [u-vnei] Pallu [Fallu] — Eliab [Eli'av].

**9** And-the-sons of [u-vnei] Eliab [Eli'av] — Nemuel [Nemu'el] and-Dathan [ve-Datan] and-Abiram [va-Aviram]. This [hu] Dathan [Datan] and-Abiram [va-Aviram], the-called-ones of [qeri'ei] the-congregation [ha-edah], who [asher] contended [hittsu] against [al] Moses [Mosheh] and-against [ve-al] Aaron [Aharon] in-the-congregation of [ba-adat] Korah [Qorakh], when-they-contended [be-hatsotam] against [al] YHWH.

> **Key terms:** קְרִיאֵי הָעֵדָה (qeri'ei ha-edah) = "the called/summoned ones of the congregation" — an ironic echo of the same phrase used honorifically in 1:16 for tribal leaders. הִצּוּ (hittsu) = "they contended/strove" — from נצה (natsah, hiphil), "to strive/quarrel." The Korah rebellion narrative is in Numbers 16. **LXX:** οὗτοι ἐπέστησαν ἐπὶ Μωυσῆν καὶ Ααρων ἐν τῇ συναγωγῇ Κορε ("these rose up against Moses and Aaron in the assembly of Korah").

**10** And-opened [va-tiftakh] the-earth [ha-arets] [et] her-mouth [piha] and-swallowed them [va-tivla' otam] and-[et] Korah [Qorakh], in-the-dying of [be-mot] the-congregation [ha-edah], when-devoured [ba-akol] the-fire [ha-esh] [et] the-five [khamishah] and-two-hundred [u-matayim] fifty [khamishim] men [ish]; and-they-became [va-yihyu] a-sign [le-nes].

> **Key terms:** וַתִּפְתַּח הָאָרֶץ אֶת־פִּיהָ (va-tiftakh ha-arets et-piha) = "and-opened the-earth her-mouth" — the personification of the earth swallowing the rebels (cf. 16:32). לְנֵס (le-nes) = "for a sign/warning" — from נֵס (nes), meaning "standard/banner/sign," here functioning as a cautionary memorial. The two hundred and fifty men burned by fire were the incense-offerers of 16:35. **LXX:** καὶ ἀνοίξασα ἡ γῆ τὸ στόμα αὐτῆς κατέπιεν αὐτοὺς καὶ Κορε ... καὶ ἐγένοντο ἐν σημείῳ ("and the earth, opening its mouth, swallowed them and Korah... and they became a sign").

**11** And-the-sons of [u-vnei] Korah [Qorakh] did-not [lo'] die [metu].

> **Key terms:** וּבְנֵי־קֹרַח לֹא־מֵתוּ (u-vnei-Qorakh lo'-metu) = "and the sons of Korah did not die" — a crucial parenthetical note. The sons of Korah survived the rebellion and became a prominent Levitical guild, credited with composing Psalms 42, 44–49, 84–85, 87–88. **LXX:** οἱ δὲ υἱοὶ Κορε οὐκ ἀπέθανον ("but the sons of Korah did not die").

---

## Simeon (vv. 12–14)

**12** The-sons of [benei] Simeon [Shim'on] by-their-clans [le-mishpekhotam]: of-Nemuel [li-Nemu'el], the-clan of [mishpakhat] the-Nemuelite [ha-Nemu'eli]; of-Jamin [le-Yamin], the-clan of [mishpakhat] the-Jaminite [ha-Yamini]; of-Jachin [le-Yakhin], the-clan of [mishpakhat] the-Jachinite [ha-Yakhini];

**13** Of-Zerah [le-Zerakh], the-clan of [mishpakhat] the-Zerahite [ha-Zarkhi]; of-Shaul [le-Sha'ul], the-clan of [mishpakhat] the-Shaulite [ha-Sha'uli].

**14** These [elleh] are the-clans of [mishpekhot] the-Simeonite [ha-Shim'oni]: two [shnayim] and-twenty [ve-esrim] thousand [elef] and-two-hundred [u-matayim].

> **Census total: twenty-two thousand two hundred (22,200).** This is the most dramatic decrease of any tribe — down from 59,300 in the first census (1:23), a loss of 37,100. The tradition links this catastrophic decline to the plague of Baal Peor, noting that Zimri son of Salu, the man slain by Phinehas (25:14), was a Simeonite chieftain. **LXX:** δύο καὶ εἴκοσι χιλιάδες καὶ διακόσιοι.

---

## Gad (vv. 15–18)

**15** The-sons of [benei] Gad [Gad] by-their-clans [le-mishpekhotam]: of-Zephon [li-Tsefon], the-clan of [mishpakhat] the-Zephonite [ha-Tsefoni]; of-Haggi [le-Khaggi], the-clan of [mishpakhat] the-Haggite [ha-Khaggi]; of-Shuni [le-Shuni], the-clan of [mishpakhat] the-Shunite [ha-Shuni];

> **Key terms:** לִצְפוֹן (li-Tsefon) — the Leningrad Codex reads צְפוֹן (Tsefon); in Gen 46:16 the same individual appears as צִפְיוֹן (Tsifyon). The LXX reads Σαφων (Saphon).

**16** Of-Ozni [le-Ozni], the-clan of [mishpakhat] the-Oznite [ha-Ozni]; of-Eri [le-Eri], the-clan of [mishpakhat] the-Erite [ha-Eri];

**17** Of-Arod [la-Arod], the-clan of [mishpakhat] the-Arodite [ha-Arodi]; of-Areli [le-Ar'eli], the-clan of [mishpakhat] the-Arelite [ha-Ar'eli].

**18** These [elleh] are the-clans of [mishpekhot] the-sons of [benei] Gad [Gad], by-their-mustered-ones [li-fqudeihem]: forty [arba'im] thousand [elef] and-five [va-khamesh] hundreds [me'ot].

> **Census total: forty thousand five hundred (40,500).** Down from 45,650 in the first census (1:25), a decrease of 5,150. **LXX:** τεσσαράκοντα χιλιάδες καὶ πεντακόσιοι.

---

## Judah (vv. 19–22)

**19** The-sons of [benei] Judah [Yehudah] — Er [Er] and-Onan [ve-Onan]; and-died [va-yamot] Er [Er] and-Onan [ve-Onan] in-the-land of [be-erets] Canaan [Kena'an].

> **Key terms:** The narrative aside recalls Gen 38:7–10: both Er and Onan died in Canaan before the descent to Egypt, so they left no clans to count. **LXX:** καὶ ἀπέθανεν Ηρ καὶ Αυναν ἐν γῇ Χανααν.

**20** And-were [va-yihyu] the-sons of [benei] Judah [Yehudah] by-their-clans [le-mishpekhotam]: of-Shelah [le-Shelah], the-clan of [mishpakhat] the-Shelanite [ha-Shelani]; of-Perez [le-Ferets], the-clan of [mishpakhat] the-Perezite [ha-Fartsi]; of-Zerah [le-Zerakh], the-clan of [mishpakhat] the-Zerahite [ha-Zarkhi].

**21** And-were [va-yihyu] the-sons of [benei] Perez [Ferets]: of-Hezron [le-Khetsron], the-clan of [mishpakhat] the-Hezronite [ha-Khetsroni]; of-Hamul [le-Khamul], the-clan of [mishpakhat] the-Hamulite [ha-Khamuli].

**22** These [elleh] are the-clans of [mishpekhot] Judah [Yehudah], by-their-mustered-ones [li-fqudeihem]: six [shishah] and-seventy [ve-shiv'im] thousand [elef] and-five [va-khamesh] hundreds [me'ot].

> **Census total: seventy-six thousand five hundred (76,500).** Up from 74,600 in the first census (1:27), an increase of 1,900. Judah remains the largest tribe. **LXX:** ἓξ καὶ ἑβδομήκοντα χιλιάδες καὶ πεντακόσιοι.

---

## Issachar (vv. 23–25)

**23** The-sons of [benei] Issachar [Yissakhar] by-their-clans [le-mishpekhotam]: of-Tola [le-Tola'], the-clan of [mishpakhat] the-Tolaite [ha-Tola'i]; of-Puvah [le-Fuvvah], the-clan of [mishpakhat] the-Punite [ha-Funi];

> **Key terms:** לְפֻוָּה (le-Fuvvah) — the Leningrad Codex reads פֻּוָּה (Fuvvah); the corresponding gentilic is הַפֻּנִי (ha-Funi, "the Punite"), showing an orthographic shift from the ancestral name to the clan designation. **LXX:** τῷ Φουα, δῆμος ὁ Φουαϊ.

**24** Of-Jashub [le-Yashuv], the-clan of [mishpakhat] the-Jashubite [ha-Yashuvi]; of-Shimron [le-Shimron], the-clan of [mishpakhat] the-Shimronite [ha-Shimroni].

**25** These [elleh] are the-clans of [mishpekhot] Issachar [Yissakhar], by-their-mustered-ones [li-fqudeihem]: four [arba'ah] and-sixty [ve-shishim] thousand [elef] and-three [u-shelosh] hundreds [me'ot].

> **Census total: sixty-four thousand three hundred (64,300).** Up from 54,400 in the first census (1:29), an increase of 9,900. **LXX:** τέσσαρες καὶ ἑξήκοντα χιλιάδες καὶ τριακόσιοι.

---

## Zebulun (vv. 26–27)

**26** The-sons of [benei] Zebulun [Zvulun] by-their-clans [le-mishpekhotam]: of-Sered [le-Sered], the-clan of [mishpakhat] the-Sardite [ha-Sardi]; of-Elon [le-Elon], the-clan of [mishpakhat] the-Elonite [ha-Eloni]; of-Jahleel [le-Yakhle'el], the-clan of [mishpakhat] the-Jahleelite [ha-Yakhle'eli].

**27** These [elleh] are the-clans of [mishpekhot] the-Zebulunite [ha-Zvuluni], by-their-mustered-ones [li-fqudeihem]: sixty [shishim] thousand [elef] and-five [va-khamesh] hundreds [me'ot].

> **Census total: sixty thousand five hundred (60,500).** Up from 57,400 in the first census (1:31), an increase of 3,100. **LXX:** ἑξήκοντα χιλιάδες καὶ πεντακόσιοι.

---

## Manasseh (vv. 28–34)

**28** The-sons of [benei] Joseph [Yosef] by-their-clans [le-mishpekhotam] — Manasseh [Menasheh] and-Ephraim [ve-Efrayim].

**29** The-sons of [benei] Manasseh [Menasheh]: of-Machir [le-Makhir], the-clan of [mishpakhat] the-Machirite [ha-Makhiri]; and-Machir [u-Makhir] begot [holid] [et] Gilead [Gil'ad]; of-Gilead [le-Gil'ad], the-clan of [mishpakhat] the-Gileadite [ha-Gil'adi].

> **Key terms:** מָכִיר הוֹלִיד אֶת־גִּלְעָד (Makhir holid et-Gil'ad) = "Machir begot Gilead" — Machir is Manasseh's sole son in this genealogy, and Gilead (both a person and a region) descends from him. The Gileadite clans become important for the Transjordan settlement (ch. 32). **LXX:** καὶ Μαχιρ ἐγέννησεν τὸν Γαλααδ.

**30** These [elleh] are the-sons of [benei] Gilead [Gil'ad]: of-Iezer [Ie'zer], the-clan of [mishpakhat] the-Iezerite [ha-Ie'zri]; of-Helek [le-Kheleq], the-clan of [mishpakhat] the-Helekite [ha-Khelqi];

> **Key terms:** אִיעֶזֶר (Ie'zer) — a shortened form of אֲבִיעֶזֶר (Avi'ezer, "my father is help"); cf. Josh 17:2, Judg 6:34 where the clan of Abiezer produces Gideon. **LXX:** Αχιεζερ, δῆμος ὁ Αχιεζερι — Vaticanus preserves the longer form.

**31** And-of-Asriel [ve-Asri'el], the-clan of [mishpakhat] the-Asrielite [ha-Asri'eli]; and-of-Shechem [ve-Shekhem], the-clan of [mishpakhat] the-Shechemite [ha-Shikhmi];

**32** And-of-Shemida [ve-Shemida'], the-clan of [mishpakhat] the-Shemidaite [ha-Shemida'i]; and-of-Hepher [ve-Khefer], the-clan of [mishpakhat] the-Hepherite [ha-Khefri].

**33** And-Zelophehad [u-Tslofkhad] son of [ben] Hepher [Khefer] had-not [lo'-hayu lo] sons [banim] but [ki im] daughters [banot]; and-the-names of [ve-shemot] the-daughters of [benot] Zelophehad [Tslofkhad] — Mahlah [Makhlah] and-Noah [ve-No'ah], Hoglah [Khoglah], Milcah [Milkah] and-Tirzah [ve-Tirtsah].

> **Key terms:** The five daughters of Zelophehad are named here — their legal case for inheritance (ch. 27) and its resolution (ch. 36) form a significant legal precedent in the Torah. The names may carry symbolic meaning: מַחְלָה (Makhlah, possibly "sickness"), נֹעָה (No'ah, "movement"), חָגְלָה (Khoglah, "partridge"), מִלְכָּה (Milkah, "queen"), and תִּרְצָה (Tirtsah, "delight/acceptance"). **LXX:** Μαλα καὶ Νουα καὶ Εγλα καὶ Μελχα καὶ Θερσα — note the LXX renders חָגְלָה as Εγλα.

**34** These [elleh] are the-clans of [mishpekhot] Manasseh [Menasheh]; and-their-mustered-ones [u-fqudeihem] — two [shnayim] and-fifty [va-khamishim] thousand [elef] and-seven [va-sheva'] hundreds [me'ot].

> **Census total: fifty-two thousand seven hundred (52,700).** Up from 32,200 in the first census (1:35), an increase of 20,500 — the largest increase of any tribe. **LXX:** δύο καὶ πεντήκοντα χιλιάδες καὶ ἑπτακόσιοι.

---

## Ephraim (vv. 35–37)

**35** These [elleh] are the-sons of [benei] Ephraim [Efrayim] by-their-clans [le-mishpekhotam]: of-Shuthelah [le-Shutelakh], the-clan of [mishpakhat] the-Shuthelahite [ha-Shutalkhi]; of-Becher [le-Bekher], the-clan of [mishpakhat] the-Bachrite [ha-Bakhri]; of-Tahan [le-Takhan], the-clan of [mishpakhat] the-Tahanite [ha-Takhani].

**36** And-these [ve-elleh] are the-sons of [benei] Shuthelah [Shutelakh]: of-Eran [le-Eran], the-clan of [mishpakhat] the-Eranite [ha-Erani].

**37** These [elleh] are the-clans of [mishpekhot] the-sons of [benei] Ephraim [Efrayim], by-their-mustered-ones [li-fqudeihem]: two [shnayim] and-thirty [u-shloshim] thousand [elef] and-five [va-khamesh] hundreds [me'ot]. These [elleh] are the-sons of [benei] Joseph [Yosef] by-their-clans [le-mishpekhotam].

> **Census total: thirty-two thousand five hundred (32,500).** Down from 40,500 in the first census (1:33), a decrease of 8,000. The verse closes the Joseph section (Manasseh + Ephraim). **LXX:** δύο καὶ τριάκοντα χιλιάδες καὶ πεντακόσιοι. οὗτοι υἱοὶ Ιωσηφ κατὰ δήμους αὐτῶν.

---

## Benjamin (vv. 38–41)

**38** The-sons of [benei] Benjamin [Binyamin] by-their-clans [le-mishpekhotam]: of-Bela [le-Vela'], the-clan of [mishpakhat] the-Belaite [ha-Val'i]; of-Ashbel [le-Ashbel], the-clan of [mishpakhat] the-Ashbelite [ha-Ashbeli]; of-Ahiram [le-Akhiram], the-clan of [mishpakhat] the-Ahiramite [ha-Akhirami];

> **Key terms:** לַאֲחִירָם (la-Akhiram) — this name appears as אֵחִי (Ekhi) in Gen 46:21. The Leningrad Codex reads אֲחִירָם (Akhiram) consistently here. **LXX:** τῷ Αχιραν, δῆμος ὁ Αχιρανι.

**39** Of-Shephupham [li-Shfufam], the-clan of [mishpakhat] the-Shuphamite [ha-Shufami]; of-Hupham [le-Khufam], the-clan of [mishpakhat] the-Huphamite [ha-Khufami].

**40** And-were [va-yihyu] the-sons of [benei] Bela [Vela'] — Ard [Ard] and-Naaman [ve-Na'aman]: of-Ard [le-Ard], the-clan of [mishpakhat] the-Ardite [ha-Ardi]; of-Naaman [le-Na'aman], the-clan of [mishpakhat] the-Naamite [ha-Na'ami].

**41** These [elleh] are the-sons of [benei] Benjamin [Binyamin] by-their-clans [le-mishpekhotam]; and-their-mustered-ones [u-fqudeihem] — five [khamishah] and-forty [ve-arba'im] thousand [elef] and-six [va-shesh] hundreds [me'ot].

> **Census total: forty-five thousand six hundred (45,600).** Up from 35,400 in the first census (1:37), an increase of 10,200. **LXX:** πέντε καὶ τεσσαράκοντα χιλιάδες καὶ ἑξακόσιοι.

---

## Dan (vv. 42–43)

**42** These [elleh] are the-sons of [benei] Dan [Dan] by-their-clans [le-mishpekhotam]: of-Shuham [le-Shukham], the-clan of [mishpakhat] the-Shuhamite [ha-Shukhami]. These [elleh] are the-clans of [mishpekhot] Dan [Dan] by-their-clans [le-mishpekhotam].

> **Key terms:** לְשׁוּחָם (le-Shukham) — Dan has only one clan listed (from a single son); in Gen 46:23 the name appears as חֻשִׁים (Khushim), a metathetic variant. **LXX:** τῷ Σαμι, δῆμος ὁ Σαμι.

**43** All [kol] the-clans of [mishpekhot] the-Shuhamite [ha-Shukhami], by-their-mustered-ones [li-fqudeihem] — four [arba'ah] and-sixty [ve-shishim] thousand [elef] and-four [ve-arba'] hundreds [me'ot].

> **Census total: sixty-four thousand four hundred (64,400).** Up from 62,700 in the first census (1:39), an increase of 1,700. Despite having only one clan, Dan is the second-largest tribe. **LXX:** τέσσαρες καὶ ἑξήκοντα χιλιάδες καὶ τετρακόσιοι.

---

## Asher (vv. 44–47)

**44** The-sons of [benei] Asher [Asher] by-their-clans [le-mishpekhotam]: of-Imnah [le-Yimnah], the-clan of [mishpakhat] the-Imnite [ha-Yimnah]; of-Ishvi [le-Yishvi], the-clan of [mishpakhat] the-Ishvite [ha-Yishvi]; of-Beriah [li-Vri'ah], the-clan of [mishpakhat] the-Beriite [ha-Vri'i].

**45** Of-the-sons of [li-vnei] Beriah [Vri'ah]: of-Heber [le-Khever], the-clan of [mishpakhat] the-Heberite [ha-Khevri]; of-Malchiel [le-Malki'el], the-clan of [mishpakhat] the-Malchielite [ha-Malki'eli].

**46** And-the-name of [ve-shem] the-daughter of [bat] Asher [Asher] — Serah [Serakh].

> **Key terms:** וְשֵׁם בַּת־אָשֵׁר שָׂרַח (ve-shem bat-Asher Serakh) = "and the name of the daughter of Asher was Serah" — one of the very few women named in the census lists (cf. Gen 46:17). The Midrash traditions assign Serah extraordinary longevity, making her a living witness from the descent to Egypt through the Exodus. **LXX:** καὶ ὄνομα θυγατρὸς Ασηρ Σαρα.

**47** These [elleh] are the-clans of [mishpekhot] the-sons of [benei] Asher [Asher], by-their-mustered-ones [li-fqudeihem] — three [sheloshah] and-fifty [va-khamishim] thousand [elef] and-four [ve-arba'] hundreds [me'ot].

> **Census total: fifty-three thousand four hundred (53,400).** Up from 41,500 in the first census (1:41), an increase of 11,900. **LXX:** τρεῖς καὶ πεντήκοντα χιλιάδες καὶ τετρακόσιοι.

---

## Naphtali (vv. 48–50)

**48** The-sons of [benei] Naphtali [Naftali] by-their-clans [le-mishpekhotam]: of-Jahzeel [le-Yakhtse'el], the-clan of [mishpakhat] the-Jahzeelite [ha-Yakhtse'eli]; of-Guni [le-Guni], the-clan of [mishpakhat] the-Gunite [ha-Guni];

**49** Of-Jezer [le-Yetser], the-clan of [mishpakhat] the-Jezerite [ha-Yitsri]; of-Shillem [le-Shillem], the-clan of [mishpakhat] the-Shillemite [ha-Shillemi].

**50** These [elleh] are the-clans of [mishpekhot] Naphtali [Naftali] by-their-clans [le-mishpekhotam]; and-their-mustered-ones [u-fqudeihem] — five [khamishah] and-forty [ve-arba'im] thousand [elef] and-four [ve-arba'] hundreds [me'ot].

> **Census total: forty-five thousand four hundred (45,400).** Down from 53,400 in the first census (1:43), a decrease of 8,000. **LXX:** πέντε καὶ τεσσαράκοντα χιλιάδες καὶ τετρακόσιοι.

---

## The Grand Total (v. 51)

**51** These [elleh] are the-mustered-ones of [pequdei] the-Sons of [benei] Israel [Yisra'el]: six [shesh] hundreds [me'ot] thousand [elef] and-a-thousand [va-elef] seven [sheva'] hundreds [me'ot] and-thirty [u-shloshim].

> **Grand census total: six hundred and one thousand seven hundred and thirty (601,730).** The first census totaled 603,550 (1:46), so the nation has decreased by 1,820 over the wilderness period — a remarkably small net decline given the repeated plagues, the judgment of chapter 14, and the deaths of the entire generation aged twenty and above at the first census. **LXX:** ἑξακόσιαι χιλιάδες καὶ χίλιοι ἑπτακόσιοι τριάκοντα.

---

## The Land Apportionment by Lot (vv. 52–56)

**52** And-spoke [va-yedabber] YHWH unto [el] Moses [Mosheh], saying [le'mor]:

**53** "To-these [la-elleh] shall-be-divided [tekhaleq] the-land [ha-arets] as-an-inheritance [be-nakhalah], by-[the]-number of [be-mispar] names [shemot]."

> **Key terms:** תֵּחָלֵק הָאָרֶץ בְּנַחֲלָה (tekhaleq ha-arets be-nakhalah) = "the land shall be divided as an inheritance" — the census is not merely military but serves as the basis for territorial apportionment. **LXX:** τούτοις μερισθήσεται ἡ γῆ κληρονομεῖν ἐξ ἀριθμοῦ ὀνομάτων.

**54** "To-the-many [la-rav] you-shall-make-much [tarbeh] his-inheritance [nakhalato], and-to-the-few [ve-la-me'at] you-shall-make-small [tam'it] his-inheritance [nakhalato]; each [ish] according-to [le-fi] his-mustered-ones [pequdav] shall-be-given [yuttan] his-inheritance [nakhalato]."

> **Key terms:** לָרַב תַּרְבֶּה נַחֲלָתוֹ וְלַמְעַט תַּמְעִיט נַחֲלָתוֹ (la-rav tarbeh nakhalato ve-la-me'at tam'it nakhalato) = "to the many you shall increase his inheritance, and to the few you shall diminish his inheritance" — proportional allotment: larger tribes receive more land. **LXX:** τοῖς πλείοσιν πλεονάσεις τὴν κληρονομίαν καὶ τοῖς ἐλάττοσιν ἐλαττώσεις τὴν κληρονομίαν αὐτῶν.

**55** "Surely [akh] by-lot [be-goral] shall-be-divided [yekhaleq] [et] the-land [ha-arets]; by-the-names of [li-shemot] the-tribes of [mattot] their-fathers [avotam] they-shall-inherit [yinkhaliu]."

> **Key terms:** אַךְ בְּגוֹרָל (akh be-goral) = "surely by lot" — the restrictive particle אַךְ (akh) emphasizes that the lot determines which tribe gets which territory; the size of the allotment is proportional (v. 54), but the location is determined by divine lot. גּוֹרָל (goral) = "lot" — the sacred casting of lots as a mechanism of divine guidance (cf. Prov 16:33). **LXX:** πλὴν κατὰ κλήρους μερισθήσεται ἡ γῆ.

**56** "By [al] the-mouth of [pi] the-lot [ha-goral] shall-be-divided [tekhaleq] his-inheritance [nakhalato], between [bein] the-many [rav] and-the-few [la-me'at]."

> **Key terms:** עַל־פִּי הַגּוֹרָל (al-pi ha-goral) = "by the mouth of the lot" — the personification of the lot as having a "mouth" that speaks YHWH's will. **LXX:** ἐκ τοῦ κλήρου μεριεῖτε τὴν κληρονομίαν αὐτῶν ἀνὰ μέσον πολλῶν καὶ ὀλίγων.

---

## The Levites (vv. 57–62)

**57** And-these [ve-elleh] are the-mustered-ones of [pequdei] the-Levite [ha-Levi] by-their-clans [le-mishpekhotam]: of-Gershon [le-Gershon], the-clan of [mishpakhat] the-Gershonite [ha-Gershunni]; of-Kohath [li-Qehat], the-clan of [mishpakhat] the-Kohathite [ha-Qehati]; of-Merari [li-Mrari], the-clan of [mishpakhat] the-Merarite [ha-Mrari].

> **Key terms:** The Levites are counted separately because they receive no territorial allotment (v. 62; cf. 18:20–24). The three Levitical divisions — Gershon, Kohath, and Merari — correspond to those in chapters 3–4. **LXX:** τῷ Γεδσων, δῆμος ὁ Γεδσωνι· τῷ Κααθ, δῆμος ὁ Κααθι· τῷ Μεραρι, δῆμος ὁ Μεραρι.

**58** These [elleh] are the-clans of [mishpekhot] Levi [Levi]: the-clan of [mishpakhat] the-Libnite [ha-Livni], the-clan of [mishpakhat] the-Hebronite [ha-Khevroni], the-clan of [mishpakhat] the-Mahlite [ha-Makhli], the-clan of [mishpakhat] the-Mushite [ha-Mushi], the-clan of [mishpakhat] the-Korahite [ha-Qorkhi]. And-Kohath [u-Qehat] begot [holid] [et] Amram [Amram].

> **Key terms:** Five Levitical clans are listed here as sub-divisions cutting across the three main branches. The note that Kohath begot Amram transitions to the Mosaic/Aaronic lineage. **LXX:** Vaticanus lists these clans similarly, adding Κορε (Korah) as a distinct clan.

**59** And-the-name of [ve-shem] the-wife of [eshet] Amram [Amram] — Jochebed [Yokheved] daughter of [bat] Levi [Levi], who [asher] bore [yaldah] [et] her [otah] to-Levi [le-Levi] in-Egypt [be-Mitsrayim]; and-she-bore [va-teled] to-Amram [le-Amram] [et] Aaron [Aharon] and-[et] Moses [Mosheh] and-[et] Miriam [Miryam] their-sister [akhotam].

> **Key terms:** יוֹכֶבֶד בַּת־לֵוִי אֲשֶׁר יָלְדָה אֹתָהּ לְלֵוִי בְּמִצְרָיִם (Yokheved bat-Levi asher yaldah otah le-Levi be-Mitsrayim) = "Jochebed daughter of Levi, whom [her mother] bore to Levi in Egypt" — this verse identifies Jochebed as a literal daughter of Levi born in Egypt, which creates a chronological tension given the 430 years in Egypt (Exod 12:40). The LXX and Samaritan Pentateuch have variant readings. Note אֹתָהּ (otah) is feminine singular, "bore her," indicating Jochebed's own birth, not her children's birth. **LXX:** ἣ ἔτεκεν τούτους τῷ Λευι ἐν Αἰγύπτῳ — Vaticanus reads τούτους ("these") instead of αὐτήν ("her"), possibly smoothing the difficulty.

**60** And-was-born [va-yivvaled] to-Aaron [le-Aharon] — Nadab [et-Nadav] and-Abihu [ve-et-Avihu'], Eleazar [et-El'azar] and-Ithamar [ve-et-Itamar].

**61** And-died [va-yamot] Nadab [Nadav] and-Abihu [va-Avihu'] in-their-bringing-near [be-haqrivam] fire [esh] strange [zarah] before [lifnei] YHWH.

> **Key terms:** בְּהַקְרִיבָם אֵשׁ זָרָה לִפְנֵי יְהוָה (be-haqrivam esh zarah lifnei YHWH) = "in their bringing near strange/unauthorized fire before YHWH" — the narrative of Lev 10:1–2. אֵשׁ זָרָה (esh zarah) = "strange fire" — fire not commanded by YHWH, the ultimate priestly transgression. **LXX:** ἐν τῷ προσφέρειν αὐτοὺς πῦρ ἀλλότριον ἔναντι κυρίου ("in their offering alien fire before the Lord").

**62** And-were [va-yihyu] their-mustered-ones [pequdeihem] three [sheloshah] and-twenty [ve-esrim] thousand [elef], every [kol] male [zakhar] from-a-son of [mi-ben] a-month [khodesh] and-upward [va-ma'lah]; for [ki] they-were-not [lo'] mustered [hotpaqdu] in-the-midst of [be-tokh] the-Sons of [benei] Israel [Yisra'el], for [ki] not [lo'] was-given [nittan] to-them [lahem] an-inheritance [nakhalah] in-the-midst of [be-tokh] the-Sons of [benei] Israel [Yisra'el].

> **Levitical total: twenty-three thousand (23,000).** Compared to 22,000 in the first Levitical census (3:39), an increase of 1,000. Levites are counted from one month old (not twenty years), as they serve YHWH rather than the military host. They receive no land inheritance because YHWH is their inheritance (18:20). **LXX:** τρεῖς καὶ εἴκοσι χιλιάδες, πᾶν ἀρσενικὸν ἀπὸ μηνιαίου καὶ ἐπάνω· οὐ γὰρ συνεπεσκέπησαν ἐν μέσῳ υἱῶν Ισραηλ, ὅτι οὐ δίδοται αὐτοῖς κλῆρος ἐν μέσῳ υἱῶν Ισραηλ.

---

## The Passing of the Wilderness Generation (vv. 63–65)

**63** These [elleh] are the-mustered-ones of [pequdei] Moses [Mosheh] and-Eleazar [ve-El'azar] the-priest [ha-kohen], who [asher] mustered [paqdu] [et] the-Sons of [benei] Israel [Yisra'el] in-the-plains of [be-arvot] Moab [Mo'av], by [al] the-Jordan of [Yarden] Jericho [Yerekho].

**64** And-among-these [u-va-elleh] there-was-not [lo'-hayah] a-man [ish] from-the-mustered-ones of [mi-pequdei] Moses [Mosheh] and-Aaron [ve-Aharon] the-priest [ha-kohen], who [asher] mustered [paqdu] [et] the-Sons of [benei] Israel [Yisra'el] in-the-wilderness of [be-midbar] Sinai [Sinai].

> **Key terms:** The comparison between the two censuses is now made explicit: not one man from the first census at Sinai remains in the second census at the plains of Moab. The entire military generation has perished, fulfilling YHWH's decree in 14:29. **LXX:** καὶ ἐν τούτοις οὐκ ἦν ἄνθρωπος τῶν ἐπεσκεμμένων ὑπὸ Μωυσῆ καὶ Ααρων ("and among these there was not a man of those mustered by Moses and Aaron").

**65** For [ki] said [amar] YHWH of-them [lahem], "They-shall-surely-die [mot yamuthu] in-the-wilderness [ba-midbar]." And-not [ve-lo'] was-left [notar] from-them [mehem] a-man [ish], except [ki im] Caleb [Kalev] son of [ben] Jephunneh [Yefunneh] and-Joshua [vi-Yehoshua'] son of [ben] Nun [Nun].

> **Key terms:** מוֹת יָמֻתוּ (mot yamuthu) = "dying they shall die" / "they shall surely die" — the infinitive absolute construction (מוֹת, mot) preceding the finite verb (יָמֻתוּ, yamuthu) creates emphatic doubling, expressing the absolute certainty of the divine decree (cf. 14:35). The chapter closes with the two exceptions: Caleb and Joshua, the faithful spies who dissented from the majority report (14:6–9, 30, 38). כִּי אִם (ki im) = "except/but only" — the restrictive particle pair marking the sole survivors. **LXX:** ὅτι εἶπεν κύριος αὐτοῖς, Θανάτῳ ἀποθανοῦνται ἐν τῇ ἐρήμῳ· καὶ οὐ κατελείφθη ἐξ αὐτῶν οὐδὲ εἷς πλὴν Χαλεβ υἱὸς Ιεφοννη καὶ Ἰησοῦς ὁ τοῦ Ναυη ("for the Lord said of them, 'By death they shall die in the wilderness'; and not one was left of them except Caleb son of Jephunneh and Joshua son of Nun") — note Θανάτῳ ἀποθανοῦνται renders the infinitive absolute construction with the cognate dative.

---

## EMET's Textual Notes

### Manuscript Condition
The Leningrad Codex pages 179–181 are in good condition with clear vocalization and cantillation marks throughout. The three-column format is consistent, and the Masoretic marginalia are visible on all three pages. Page 179 contains the end of Numbers 25 and the beginning of chapter 26; the parashah Phinehas (פִּינְחָס) begins at 25:10 and extends through 30:1 (29:40 in some traditions), encompassing the entirety of chapter 26. The census lists are written continuously without significant spacing between tribal units, though the Masoretic paragraph divisions (setumah markers, ס) appear between several tribal sections.

### Key Textual Observations

1. **Infinitive Absolute (v. 65):** The construction מוֹת יָמֻתוּ (mot yamuthu) is rendered "they shall surely die" with the doubling convention. The Vaticanus LXX preserves this emphasis with Θανάτῳ ἀποθανοῦνται (the cognate dative construction).

2. **Clan Name Formulae:** The chapter uses a rigid formula: לְ + proper name, מִשְׁפַּחַת הַ + gentilic. The gentilic suffix is consistently -י (-i) throughout the Leningrad Codex. The LXX renders מִשְׁפָּחָה as δῆμος ("people/district") rather than φυλή ("tribe"), reserving φυλή for מַטֶּה.

3. **Simeon's Dramatic Decline (v. 14):** The tribe of Simeon drops from 59,300 to 22,200 — a loss of 37,100 (approximately 63%). No explanation is given in the text itself, but the juxtaposition with the Baal Peor narrative and the identification of Zimri as a Simeonite chief (25:14) provides the narrative logic.

4. **Zelophehad's Daughters (v. 33):** The insertion of this narrative note within the Manassite clan list prepares for the legal case of chapter 27. The five daughters are named — an unusual feature in census lists that signals their legal and theological importance.

5. **Jochebed's Birth (v. 59):** The phrase אֲשֶׁר יָלְדָה אֹתָהּ לְלֵוִי בְּמִצְרָיִם ("whom she bore to Levi in Egypt") presents a significant chronological question. If Jochebed is literally Levi's daughter and Moses' mother, the sojourn in Egypt must be considerably shorter than 430 years. The LXX Vaticanus appears to read τούτους ("these," i.e., Aaron, Moses, Miriam) instead of αὐτήν ("her"), reflecting an early attempt to smooth this difficulty.

6. **Korah's Sons (v. 11):** The parenthetical note that "the sons of Korah did not die" stands as a redemptive counterpoint within the Reubenite genealogy. The survival of Korah's line is theologically significant — they become temple singers and psalmists (Pss 42–49, 84–85, 87–88).

7. **Census Comparison Summary:**

| Tribe | First Census (Num 1) | Second Census (Num 26) | Change |
|-------|---------------------|----------------------|--------|
| Reuben | 46,500 | 43,730 | -2,770 |
| Simeon | 59,300 | 22,200 | -37,100 |
| Gad | 45,650 | 40,500 | -5,150 |
| Judah | 74,600 | 76,500 | +1,900 |
| Issachar | 54,400 | 64,300 | +9,900 |
| Zebulun | 57,400 | 60,500 | +3,100 |
| Manasseh | 32,200 | 52,700 | +20,500 |
| Ephraim | 40,500 | 32,500 | -8,000 |
| Benjamin | 35,400 | 45,600 | +10,200 |
| Dan | 62,700 | 64,400 | +1,700 |
| Asher | 41,500 | 53,400 | +11,900 |
| Naphtali | 53,400 | 45,400 | -8,000 |
| **Total** | **603,550** | **601,730** | **-1,820** |
| Levi | 22,000 | 23,000 | +1,000 |

8. **LXX Divergences in Proper Names:** Several clan names show orthographic variation between the MT and the Vaticanus LXX: Tsefon/Saphon (v. 15), Ie'zer/Achiezer (v. 30), Shukham/Sami (v. 42). These likely reflect variant Vorlage traditions rather than translation choices.

9. **Parashah Structure:** Numbers 26 falls entirely within the parashah Phinehas (פִּינְחָס, 25:10–30:1). The open parashah (פתוחה) marker is visible in the Leningrad Codex before the census command, and closed parashah (סתומה) markers separate several tribal sections.
